design_principle: The conversation never resets. Context accumulates across all turns. Behavioral workflows activate based on detected intent and context state, all inside the same continuous session.

rag_core_pattern.description: Applied in WF2 and WF3. The retrieval query is never just the raw user message. It is always enriched by the accumulated decision context. Evaluation gates enforce retrieval quality and response quality before proceeding.

workflows.name: Conversational Response Loop
workflows.description: Master loop executed for every user message. Classifies intent, updates context, retrieves knowledge, and generates a response. Orchestrates all secondary workflows.
workflows.entry: User sends a message with a session identifier
workflows.exit: Assistant response and context snapshot returned to caller

transitions.description: User message arrives with session identifier
System determines the type of interaction (maps to FR2)
Signals extracted and decision context enriched (activates WF1)
Retrieval query built from intent + accumulated context, not raw message (maps to FR4)
Relevant content fetched from appropriate knowledge source (activates WF2 or WF3)
context_recall_score and context_precision_score computed on RetrievedEvidence. If recall < threshold, query is refined and retrieval retried before proceeding.
Response produced using retrieved evidence and decision context
groundedness_score and relevance_score computed on AssistantResponse. If either < threshold, response is blocked and a safe fallback returned.
Validated assistant response and context snapshot delivered to caller

workflows.name: Decision Context Building
workflows.description: Runs inside every turn. Progressively builds a structured understanding of user preferences, constraints, and fundraising intent from conversation signals.
workflows.pre_condition: At session start, DecisionContext is already seeded with the user's school (from DB), prior conversation signals (from ConversationHistory if any), and purchase signals (from PurchaseHistory if populated). WF1 enriches this context further with each new user turn.
workflows.purpose: Progressively understand user needs, preferences, and fundraising intent

transitions.description: New user message available for analysis
Preferences and constraints identified from the message
Decision context enriched with newly extracted signals
System checks whether the context is sufficient for a recommendation
One targeted question asked when gaps exist. This step is optional.
Sufficient context available to activate product recommendation workflow

example.clarification: Are you looking for snacks or breakfast items?

workflows.name: Product Recommendation
workflows.description: Guides the user toward a personalized product decision grounded in the accumulated decision context and retrieved product data.
workflows.trigger: product_inquiry intent OR decision context is ready (WF1 exited with context ready)
workflows.purpose: Guide the user toward a personalized product decision

transitions.description: Decision context checked for sufficient preference signals
Query built from decision context (e.g., 'healthy snacks for picky children'), not raw user message
Product catalog queried with enriched query; top results returned
context_recall_score and context_precision_score computed on RetrievedEvidence. If recall < threshold, query is refined and retrieval retried.
Retrieved products (with passing quality scores) passed as grounded context into the generation step
Personalized recommendation produced citing only retrieved products, with explicit connection to the user's school fundraising goal
Response structured with product suggestions and preference-aligned reasoning

example.retrieval_query: healthy snacks suitable for picky children
example.products_retrieved: top matching items from product catalog
example.response: These healthy snack options work well for picky kids: [retrieved products]

workflows.name: Referral Guidance
workflows.description: Explains the referral program mechanics and encourages the user to take a referral action to increase school fundraising.
workflows.trigger: referral_question intent OR fundraising support opportunity detected in conversation
workflows.purpose: Explain the referral program mechanics and encourage the user to take action

transitions.description: User signals interest in helping their school or in referrals
Query built from intent and fundraising context signals
Platform-specific referral rules fetched from knowledge base
context_recall_score and context_precision_score computed on RetrievedEvidence. If recall < threshold, query is refined and retrieval retried.
Retrieved rules (with passing quality scores) passed as grounded context into the generation step
Explanation built strictly from retrieved rules, not LLM memory
Specific referral action recommended based on retrieved eligibility rules
Guidance and suggested action delivered to the user

example.user_message: How do I help my school more?
example.retrieval_query: referral program rules school donation bonus
example.rules_retrieved: referral eligibility, bonus mechanics, invitation flow
example.response: Inviting other parents increases school donations. Here is how the referral program works: [retrieved rules]

workflow_interaction.model: All secondary workflows run inside the primary loop. A single user message activates WF1 always, plus one or more additional workflows depending on detected intent.

routing_rules.condition: product_inquiry intent OR decision context is ready
referral_question intent OR fundraising opportunity detected

workflow_interaction.note: WF2 and WF3 can both activate in the same turn if the user message contains both product and referral signals.
